title: camissonia boothii subsp. rutila
intro: Camissonia boothii subsp. rutila, also known as the desert evening primrose, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northwestern Mexico. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, it has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Camissonia boothii subsp. rutila include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve drying the flowers and leaves to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to support overall health and well-being.
